description:
You are acting as a junior log–analyst who has just received a static process listing that was taken from an embedded device.  
    The raw snapshot is already stored on the machine so that you do NOT need root privileges or live-system access.

    1. Source data  
       • File: /home/user/data/process_snapshot.txt  
       • The file is plain text and is already present.  
       • It contains a single header line followed by one line per running process (PID, USER and COMMAND; columns are separated by one or more blanks).  
         You MUST NOT modify this file.

    2. Your goal  
       • Produce a new summary file called /home/user/data/user_proc_count.log.  
       • Create it with ONE single terminal command (you may of course use pipes, redirections and subshells inside that one command).  
       • The log must list, for every distinct user name that appears in “/home/user/data/process_snapshot.txt”, the count of how many processes that user owns.

    3. Mandatory output format (what the test-suite will check)  
       • One user per line.  
       • Exact pattern per line: <username>:<count> (all lowercase, no surrounding blanks).  
       • Lines **must** be sorted alphabetically by user name (e.g. daemon before root before user).  
       • No header line, no trailing blank lines.  
       • A single trailing newline at the very end of the file is required.

       Example of the required layout (your file must reproduce these exact three lines because the snapshot is fixed):
       daemon:2
       root:5
       user:4

    4. Verification hint  
       The automated grader will:
       a) read /home/user/data/user_proc_count.log  
       b) compare it byte-for-byte against the expected three-line text above.

    5. Constraints  
       • You must generate /home/user/data/user_proc_count.log with ONLY ONE compound command.  
       • Do not hard-code the counts; derive them from the provided snapshot.  
       • Do not use temporary files in other directories; write directly to the target file.

    Once you have created /home/user/data/user_proc_count.log exactly as described, the task is complete.
truth:
Pre-existing file that is already present at task start:
    -----------------------------------------------------------------
    Path: /home/user/data/process_snapshot.txt  
    Permissions: 0644, owned by user:user  
    Contents (including leading spaces; each line ended by a single LF):
        PID USER     COMMAND
          1 root     systemd
          2 root     kthreadd
        100 daemon   some_daemon
        101 daemon   another_daemon
        200 user     bash
        201 user     vim
        202 user     python
        300 user     java
        400 root     sshd
        401 root     sshd
        402 root     cron

    Expected file that the agent must create:
    -----------------------------------------------------------------
    Path: /home/user/data/user_proc_count.log  
    Permissions after creation: 0644, owned by user:user (default umask)  
    Required contents (exactly three lines, each terminated by LF, no extra spaces):
    daemon:2
    root:5
    user:4

    The grader will load the file and perform an exact string comparison with the block above.
name: task_000000_4cd2c445
